_draft.note: lens/contradiction have no legacy source; take is a mechanical concat scaffold. Author from concept-lens-vocabulary.md before promoting to gold.

read.image: neon, and no one to call
read.take: Chasing a high, a city, a person, anything to outrun being alone at night. The euphoria is real and so is the emptiness underneath it. He floors it through the dark and hopes the motion will pass for connection. It sprints. The synths flood in like headlights on a wet road, gorgeous and a little desperate.

arc.label: Intro
arc.scene: The synth hits and the song is already at full speed, no warmup, like flooring it the second the light turns.

arc.label: Verses
arc.scene: Under all the gloss he is just asking someone to be there. The need keeps peeking through the shine.

arc.label: Outro
arc.scene: The lights keep flashing but the feeling never arrives. He is still driving, still alone.

lines.line: I can't sleep until I feel your touch
lines.insight: Insomnia dressed up as devotion. The glamour barely covers how plainly he needs someone.
The thing that thrills him is the thing keeping him from seeing clearly. The high is also the blindfold.

read.texture: Glossy retro synthwave with a pulse you feel in your chest. Bright, propulsive, relentlessly forward, all chrome and cold night air.

themes.name: Speed as avoidance
themes.description: The faster the song moves, the clearer it gets that he is running from a quiet he cannot sit in.

themes.name: City light as substitute
themes.description: The glow of the strip stands in for the warmth of a person who is not picking up the phone.
